name: Nairobi Integrated Flood Risk Management Plan
blocking_factor: Not gazetted — stalled at county assembly
notes: Comprehensive plan covering all 85 wards. Drainage master plan component designed with World Bank support. County assembly has not adopted it. New governor's administration reviewing.

name: Mathare River Riparian Enforcement
blocking_factor: Political resistance — enforcement requires demolition of 4000+ structures
notes: 30m riparian buffer consistently violated. Over 40000 people live within the buffer zone. NCC has issued enforcement orders annually since 2016. None executed. Pre-election cycle enforcement is impossible.

name: Kibera Stormwater Drainage Upgrade
blocking_factor: Land tenure disputes blocking Phase 2 alignment
notes: Phase 1 (main collectors) complete 2022. Phase 2 requires re-routing through disputed land. Secure tenure is prerequisite for infrastructure investment in informal settlements.

name: Mukuru Special Planning Area Drainage
blocking_factor: Budget absorption and resettlement budget shortfall
notes: Mukuru SPA gazette 2019 enabled investment. Drainage design complete. Implementation requires relocation of structures on drainage easements. Compensation budget is 40% of requirement.

name: Mathare Valley Flood Early Warning
blocking_factor: Last-mile SMS penetration in Mathare informal settlement
notes: Community alert system operational in Mathare 4A and 4B. Sections 1-3 not covered. Renter population not reached by alert chain anchored to structure owners.

name: Korogocho Drainage and Flood Protection
notes: Phase 1 procurement completed 2021. Contractor abandoned site 2022. Re-procurement delayed. Korogocho remains highest flood risk zone in Nairobi after Mathare.

name: Nairobi River Rehabilitation
notes: 18km of river cleaned and partially rehabilitated 2015-2020. Industrial effluent discharge from upper Nairobi River unresolved. Downstream communities bear full pollution and flood burden.

name: Ward-Level MCAs Flood Accountability
blocking_factor: Political will — accountability mechanism requires MCAs to report on constituents
notes: Proposal to integrate flood incident reporting into MCA quarterly reports. Not adopted. Would create individual political accountability at ward level for 85 sitting MCAs.

name: NCC Planning Approval Flood Risk Check
blocking_factor: NCC planning department not cross-referencing flood risk maps
notes: Flood risk map published 2020. Planning approvals in flood zones documented annually. No mandatory cross-reference process established. Developers exploit gap.

name: Ngong River Flood Retention Ponds
blocking_factor: Land acquisition in Kibera and Lang'ata
notes: Three retention pond sites identified. Two are on land with unresolved tenure. One site acquired but construction not commenced. Budget eroded by 7 years of inflation.
